name.text: Calculating slant height of the original cone and volume a frustum

questiontext.text:
A solid right circular cone has a base radius of {@R@} cm and a vertical height of {@H@} cm. A smaller cone is cut off from the top to leave a frustum of height {@h_frustum@} cm and top radius {@r@} cm as shown below.

[[jsxgraph width="400px" height="350px"]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, { /* Expanded boundingbox: [left, top, right, bottom] */ boundingbox: [-7, 12, 7, -3], axis: false, showCopyright: false }); var R = {#Rdraw#}; var r = {#rdraw#}; var h = {#hdraw#}; var H = {#Hdraw#}; /* 1. Points */ var pBottomLeft = board.create('point', [-R, 0], {visible: false}); var pBottomRight = board.create('point', [R, 0], {visible: false}); var pTopLeft = board.create('point', [-r, h], {visible: false}); var pTopRight = board.create('point', [r, h], {visible: false}); var apex = board.create('point', [0, H], {visible: false}); /* 2. Frustum Sides */ board.create('segment', [pBottomLeft, pTopLeft], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 2}); board.create('segment', [pBottomRight, pTopRight], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 2}); /* 3. Top Ellipse (Solid) */ board.create('curve', [ function(t){ return r * Math.cos(t); }, function(t){ return h + (r * 0.25) * Math.sin(t); }, 0, 2 * Math.PI ], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 2, fillColor: '#f0f0f0', fillOpacity: 0.3}); /* 4. Bottom Ellipse (Front solid, Back dashed) */ board.create('curve', [ function(t){ return R * Math.cos(t); }, function(t){ return (R * 0.25) * Math.sin(t); }, Math.PI, 2 * Math.PI ], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 2}); board.create('curve', [ function(t){ return R * Math.cos(t); }, function(t){ return (R * 0.25) * Math.sin(t); }, 0, Math.PI ], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 1, dash: 2}); /* 5. "Ghost" Lines to Apex */ board.create('segment', [pTopLeft, apex], {dash: 2, strokeColor: 'gray'}); board.create('segment', [pTopRight, apex], {dash: 2, strokeColor: 'gray'}); /* 6. Indicators and Labels */ // Height (Red) board.create('segment', [[0, 0], [0, H]], {dash: 1, strokeColor: 'red'}); board.create('text', [-2.5, H/2, "{#H#} cm"], {color: 'red', fontSize: 14, fontWeight: 'bold'}); // Radius (Blue) board.create('segment', [[0, 0], [R, 0]], {strokeColor: 'blue', strokeWidth: 2}); board.create('text', [R/2, -1.2, "{#R#} cm"], {color: 'blue', fontSize: 14, fontWeight: 'bold'}); [[/jsxgraph]]

(a) Calculate the slant height (L) of the original full cone.

Slant height = [[input:ans1]] [[validation:ans1]][[feedback:prt1]]

(b) Calculate the volume of the remaining frustum.

Volume = [[input:ans2]] [[validation:ans2]][[feedback:prt2]]

generalfeedback.text:
Worked solution

Part (a): Slant Height of the Original Cone

Using Pythagoras' Theorem: \[ L^2 = R^2 + H^2 \] \[ L^2 = {@R@}^2 + {@H@}^2 = {@R^2 + H^2@} \] \[ L = \sqrt{{@R^2 + H^2@}}\]

Part (b): Volume of the Frustum

We use the formula: \( V = \frac{1}{3}\pi h(R^2 + Rr + r^2) \)

Substituting our values:
\( R = {@R@} \), \( r = {@r@} \), \( h = {@h_frustum@} \)

\( \begin{align} V &= \frac{1}{3} \times \pi \times {@h_frustum@} \times ({@R^2@} + {@R*r@} + {@r^2@}) \\ V &= \frac{1}{3} \times \pi \times {@h_frustum@} \times {@R^2 + R*r + r^2@} \\ V &= {@ta@} \text{ cm}^3 \end{align}\)

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.

node.description: Checks for the correct value of the slight height
Checks for the correct volume of the frustum
